subject: DevOps
title: Cloudflare DNS Routing Leak (2022)
description: A quick win: understand Cloudflare DNS Routing Leak (2022).

hook.text: June 2022. A BGP misconfiguration routed Cloudflare traffic through the wrong networks.

buildup.text: A change in 19 data centers triggered a broader BGP issue. Traffic was misrouted globally.

discovery.text: Cloudflare sites went offline or slowed. Users saw 500 errors across many popular websites.

twist.text: The fix was deployed in 87 minutes. But the incident showed how fragile internet routing is.

climax.text: Cloudflare improved their change procedures and added BGP prefix validation checks.

punchline.text: BGP is fragile. One wrong route can break the internet.

quiz.question: What fundamental internet protocol was involved in Cloudflare's 2022 issue?
options: BGP, the protocol that routes traffic between networks
HTTP, the protocol for web requests
DNS, the domain name resolution protocol
